titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Kay Ryan Papers
titlestmt.author: by Emma Gronbeck

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday October 20, 2021 at 04:01

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Kay Ryan papers contains correspondence, writings, artwork, photographs, personal and professional papers, poetry broadsides and other printed material, and audiovisual and computer media documenting the life and career of American poet Kay Ryan. The bulk of the collection consists of individual and collected poems, prose, notes, and journals, as well as correspondence between Ryan and her friends, family, colleagues, publishers, arts organizations such as the Poetry Foundation, and the Library of Congress. Notable correspondents include Billy Collins, Dana Gioia, Jane Hirshfield, David Lehman, Sarah Lindsay, Samuel Menashe, Rosalie Moore, and Atsuro Riley. Works represented in the collection include Dragon Acts to Dragon Ends , Strangely Marked Metal , and Say Uncle . Additionally included are records of grants and awards, letters to Ryan during her tenure as United States Poet Laureate (2008-2010), teaching materials, and memorabilia. The records span from 1945 to 2017, with the bulk of the material falling between 1973 and 2017.

bioghist.head: Kay Ryan (1945-)
bioghist.p: Kay Ryan is an American poet, writer and educator. She served as the sixteenth United States Poet Laureate (2008-2010) and won a Pulitzer Prize for her collection The Best of It: New and Selected Poems (2010).

Ryan was born on September 21, 1945 in San Jose, California and raised in the San Joaquin Valley. She received both a B.A. and M.A. in English from the University of California, Los Angeles in 1967 and 1968, respectively. In 1971, she began teaching remedial English part-time at the College of Marin in Kentfield, California, which she would continue for over 30 years. Ryan is the author of nine books of poetry, beginning with her self-published debut, Dragon Acts to Dragon Ends (1983), and her first commercially published collection, Strangely Marked Metal (1985). Other works include Flamingo Watching (1994), Elephant Rocks (1996), Say Uncle (2000), The Niagara River (2005), Jam Jar Lifeboat and Other Novelties Exposed (2008), and Erratic Facts (2015). Ryan's poetry has been featured in publications such as The Atlantic , The New York Times , The Paris Review , and The Yale Review , as well as numerous anthologies, including four volumes of The Best American Poetry . Ryan most recently published her first collection of essays, Synthesizing Gravity: Selected Prose (2020).

In addition to the Pulitzer Prize, Ryan's awards include the National Humanities Medal, the Ruth Lilly Poetry Prize, the Union League Poetry Prize, and three Pushcart Prizes. She has also been the recipient of fellowships from The John D. and Catherine T. MacArthur Foundation, The John Simon Guggenheim Memorial Foundation, The National Endowment for the Arts, and The Ingram Merrill Foundation. She served as Chancellor of the Academy of American Poets from 2006 to 2013.

Since 1971, Ryan has lived in Marin County, California. She was married to the late Carol Adair, a fellow instructor at the College of Marin, who was her partner from 1978 until Adair's death in 2009.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The Kay Ryan papers contains correspondence, writings, artwork, photographs, personal and professional papers, poetry broadsides and other printed material, and audiovisual and computer media documenting the life and career of American poet Kay Ryan. The bulk of the collection consists of individual and collected poems, prose, notes, and journals, as well as correspondence between Ryan and her friends, family, colleagues, publishers, arts organizations such as the Poetry Foundation, and the Library of Congress. Notable correspondents include Billy Collins, Dana Gioia, Jane Hirshfield, David Lehman, Sarah Lindsay, Samuel Menashe, Rosalie Moore, and Atsuro Riley. Works represented in the collection include Dragon Acts to Dragon Ends , Strangely Marked Metal , and Say Uncle . Additionally included are records of grants and awards, letters to Ryan during her tenure as United States Poet Laureate (2008-2010), teaching materials, and memorabilia. The records span from 1945 to 2017, with the bulk of the material falling between 1973 and 2017.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into six series: I. Correspondence, 1973-2016. II. Writings, 1973-2017. III. Personal and Professional Papers, 1945-2017. IV. Visual Materials, 1983-2017. V. Printed Material, 1961-2016. VI. Computer and Audiovisual Media, 1994-2009.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit. Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing. This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 40 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 41 (audiovisual media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copy may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 42 (student records): Restricted until 2085. For further information, consult appropriate curator.
Box 43 (legal, medical, and financial records): Restricted until 2035. For further information, consult appropriate curator.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Kay Ryan Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Glenn Horowitz Bookseller, Inc., on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2018.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Kay Ryan Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains professional and personal correspondence between Ryan and her friends, family, colleagues, and publishers, as well as government agencies and nonprofit organizations. Also included is a comprehensive collection of envelopes and correspondence documenting years of poetry submissions to various publications.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: General Correspondence, and Poetry Submissions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Individual correspondents include former poet laureate Billy Collins, New Haven artist Jan Cunningham, and fellow poets and writers Dana Gioia, Rachel Hadas, Jane Hirshfield, David Lehman, Sarah Lindsay, Samuel Menashe, Rosalie Moore, Atsuro Riley, May Sarton, and Judy Stone. Organizations and publications represented in the series include The Atlantic Monthly , the College of Marin, Copper Beech Press, Grove Atlantic, the Library of Congress, The New Yorker , the Poetry Foundation, Pushcart Press, The Threepenny Review , The Yale Review , and Zyzzyva . Frequently enclosed with letters are writings, clippings, photographs, drawings, interview transcripts, publishing permissions and agreements, financial records, and ephemera.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by correspondent, with unsorted correspondence, fan mail, and letters of congratulations filed at the end of the sequence.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Correspondence can also be found in Series II. Writings, Series III. Personal and Professional Papers, and Series IV. Visual Materials.

did.unittitle: 92nd Street Y (New York, N.Y.), Unterberg Poetry Center
City Literary Institute (London, England) (with Poems in the Waiting Room pamphlets)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kay Ryan's friend from the University of California at Irvine.

title.part: The New York Times Book Review

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kay Ryan's mother.

scopecontent.p: Kay Ryan's cousin.

did.unittitle: South Coast Poetry Journal (California State University, Fullerton. English Department)

scopecontent.p: Kay Ryan's grandchild.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.p: Kay Ryan's grandchild.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kay Ryan's stepdaughter.

did.unittitle: The Southern Review (Louisiana State University Press)
The Writer's Almanac (Prairie Home Productions LLC)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of Ryan's collection of returned poetry submissions. Some envelopes contain correspondence, handwritten notes, or poems, while others are now empty. Publications to which Ryan submitted her poems include The American Scholar , The Atlantic Monthly , The Chicago Review , The Georgia Review , The Kenyon Review , The New Yorker , The Paris Review , Poetry , The Threepenny Review , The Yale Review , and Zyzzyva .

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Additional notes and correspondence related to poetry submissions can be found in Series II. Writings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains individual and collected poems, prose, notes and notebooks, diaries and journals, financial records, and materials related to poetry readings and speaking engagements.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into six subseries: Poetry Collections, Individual Poems, Taylor Street Press, Prose, Notes and Journals, and Public Readings and Events.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Additional correspondence related to writings and publications can be found in Series I. Correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains materials related to Ryan's published and unpublished books of poetry, as well as other groupings of poetry created by Ryan. Included are contracts, correspondence, drafts, proofs, book reviews, press materials, and illustrations for works such as Ryan's self-published debut collection, Dragon Acts to Dragon Ends and Face Up , a collection of poems inspired by the cards of the tarot, as well as Strangely Marked Metal , Say Uncle , and Flamingo Watching .

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by published title, or what is assumed to be the final title, with untitled collections filed at the end of the sequence.

A Certain Kind of Eden , typescript
Do Not Address the Blade , typescript
Elephant Rocks , contract with Grove Atlantic

title.part: Odd Blocks: Selected and New Poems

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains holograph, typescript, and typescript printout individual poems spanning from 1973 to 2017.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by published title or what is assumed to be the final title. Untitled poems are filed at the end of the sequence and organized alphabetically by the first line of the poem.

did.unittitle: "Absence," holograph and printout from digital file
"After Hearing the Poet, Czeslaw Milosz," typescript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a note by Ryan: "first poem I wrote when I was 19."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of a metal Marin County toolbox containing papers and objects associated with Ryan and Carol Adair's self-publishing enterprise, Taylor Street Press, under which Ryan published her first book of poetry, Dragon Acts to Dragon Ends .

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: Dragon Acts to Dragon Ends (3 copies)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains essays, book and poetry reviews, notes for lectures, workshops and presentations, and other prose writings by Ryan. Also included are interviews with Ryan conducted by others.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: "Oddly Jolly," review of The Newspaper of Claremont Street by Elizabeth Jolley (for The Threepenny Review ), holograph, typescript, and clippings

Commentary for Best of the Best: A Poetic Chrestomathy , printout from digital file
Review of The Measured Word: On Poetry and Science , holograph and printout from digital file
"Profound Lightness," review of This Craft of Verse by Jorge Luis Borges (for The Threepenny Review ), holograph, printout from digital file, and clippings
"An Interview With Marin Poet Kay Ryan" by Gray Giles, clippings
"Poetry in Review," review of The Poems of Marianne Moore (for The Yale Review ), holograph, printout from digital file, and clippings
"Lyrical Intellect: An Interview with Kay Ryan" by Marit MacArthur, printout from digital file
Introduction for City Arts and Lectures at the Herbst Theater, printout from digital file
"Donning the Mantle," keynote address notes for Napa Valley Writers Conference, holograph and printout from digital file
"I Go to AWP," printout from digital file and clippings
Letter to the editor, "Kudos for Kipen," clipping
"Memory and Forgetting," printout from digital file
Review of Natural History by Dan Chiasson, holograph and printout from digital file
"Biography of Early 'Career'" (for Jane Hirshfield), printout from digital file
"William Bronk," holograph and printout from digital file
"A Consideration of Poetry," holograph, printout from digital file, and clippings
"Valentine's Day piece for Poetry ," printout from digital file
Essay on Floating City by Anne Pierson Wiese for Whitman Award ceremony, printout from digital file
Blog entries for Poetry Foundation, printout from digital file
Emily Dickinson talk for Branching Out
Research materials, clippings and printout from digital file
Notes and handouts, printout from digital file
Notes on writing for "The Poet's View Series" (Annals of American Poets), holograph
"I Demand to Speak with God," review of The Notebooks of Robert Frost (for Poetry ), holograph and printout from digital file
Notes for "Clarity and Obscurity" conference panel, holograph and research materials
Notes for Stevie Smith talk at Stanford University
Notes for Poets Forum, printout from digital file
"Tenth Muse," introductions for participants at the 92nd Street Y, Unterberg Poetry Center, holograph and printout from digital file
Interview questions for new edition of Literature and Intro to Poetry , printout from digital file
"Cooling the Surface, Tending the Cracks: An Interview with Kay Ryan" by Jane Collins, printout from digital file
"Kay Ryan: An Interview" by Grace Cavalieri ( The American Poetry Review , full issue)
"The Edges of Time," printout from digital file
Notes for "How Poetry Works" panel at Key West
Interview with Jane Collins, holograph and correspondence
Notes for Library of Congress Poetry Day video simulcast, printout from digital file
Notes on "Spring and Fall, to a young child" by Gerard Manley Hopkins, holograph
Review of "We Met at the End of the Party" by Philip Larkin, printout from digital file
Notes for Stanford University dorm conversation, holograph
Talk about funniness in poetry using [Philip] Larkin's "Sad Steps," holograph and printout from digital file
Notes on Emily Dickinson's poetry (for a conversation with Robert Hass), holograph
Notes on "Declarations of Independence" by Dennis O'Driscoll ( Dark Horse , volume 28), holograph
"Kay Ryan Interview" by Andy Kuhn, printout from digital file
"How Much Can You Take Away?" printout from digital file
"Specks," printout from digital file and clippings
"William Carlos Williams, reading at the 92nd Street Y Poetry Center, Jan. 1954," printout from digital file
Notes for "Litquake" Poetry and Science panel, holograph
Notes for Emily Dickinson talk, University of California San Francisco, holograph and correspondence
Review of Lila by Marilynne Robinson
Notes for "Rhyme Talk," Hugo House, Seattle
Notes for "Rhyme Class," Hugo House, Seattle
"Stumble Bones by Poe Dismuke," printout from digital file
"Fit meat for a hungry mind," printout from digital file and clippings
"To Arrive Somewhere - A Conversation with Kay Ryan" by Gail Yngve, printout from digital file
"An Interview with Kay Ryan" by Kay Ryan, illustrated typescript
"Thinking About Stevie Smith," printout from digital file

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains poem submission logs, poem lists, poetry profit notes, and unsorted notes, as well as notebooks, journals, unbound journal entries, memo booklets, planners, and calendars.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by file title.

did.unittitle: Submissions, "B: Scholar" [ The American Scholar ?], typescript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains posters, pamphlets, announcements, and other ephemera from public poetry readings and talks at which Ryan presented or participated, as well as her handwritten notes for each event. A small amount of correspondence, receipts, tickets, and itineraries are also included.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: Phi Beta Kappa, Literary Exercises at Harvard University
Poets and Writers Live, San Francisco

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains awards, correspondence, diplomas, teaching materials, grant applications, and papers related to Ryan's appointment as United States Poet Laureate, as well as memorabilia, including a collection of stick puppets and a handmade metal wall-hanging picturing Frieda Kahlo.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by file or object title.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Additional personal and professional correspondence and records are located in Series I. Correspondence.

did.unittitle: Academy of American Poets, Walt Whitman Award, correspondence and notes
Emily Dickinson Museum, Tell It Slant Award, correspondence and ephemera
Maurice English Poetry Award, correspondence and ephemera
The National Book Critics Circle, correspondence and ephemera
National Humanities Medal, correspondence and ephemera
Northern California Book Awards, correspondence and certificates
Ruth Lilly Poetry Prize, correspondence and ephemera
Diploma, University of California Los Angeles (Kay Lucille Pedersen)
Diploma, University of California Los Angeles (Kay Pedersen Ryan)
The John D. and Catherine T. MacArthur Foundation, MacArthur Fellowship, certificate
The John D. and Catherine T. MacArthur Foundation, MacArthur Fellowship, correspondence
The John Simon Guggenheim Memorial Foundation, correspondence and applications
The John Simon Guggenheim Memorial Foundation, correspondence and applications
National Endowment for the Arts, rejections
Drawing, "Focalization No. III," by Reed Maxson (photocopy)
Ephemera from Kay Ryan's study wall
Ephemera from Kay Ryan's study wall, photographs
Ephemera, wedding of Barbara Nylund Morgan and Wendyn Cadden
Handmade hamsa wall-hanging with portrait of Frieda Kahlo

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 13 ID cards for various speaking events, some with lanyards.

did.unittitle: "Say Uncle," translation project booklet (La Guardia Community College)
Washington D.C. Cosmos Club, metal bookmark
Stanford University, notes," classes by week"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of sketches, drawings, and paintings, as well as photographs of Ryan and her colleagues, friends, and family.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Works of Art on Paper, and Photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains sketches, sketchbooks, drawings, paintings, a collection of Christmas cards illustrated by Ryan, and a deck of hand-drawn tarot cards.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by file title.

did.unittitle: Untitled drawing of a man with a horse-drawn wagon
Untitled painting of dragons in a gondola (with inscription "For [Shandy?]")
Untitled painting of a plate of apples on a table
Untitled painting of a teapot on a table
Untitled painting of a tower on an island

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains photographs of Ryan, as well as her colleagues, friends, and family, including Carol Adair and Atsuro Riley. The majority of photographs are images of Ryan attending public events or portraits taken for publications.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by file title.

did.unittitle: Kay Ryan, final reading in Washington D.C. (taken by Marsha Pedersen)
Kay Ryan at City Arts and Lectures, San Francisco
Kay Ryan at City Arts and Lectures, San Francisco (taken by Sydney Goldstein)
Kay Ryan at City Arts and Lectures, San Francisco
Kay Ryan at the Lannan Foundation, with Atsuro Riley (taken by Don J. Usner)
Kay Ryan at readings and events
Kay Ryan receiving the National Humanities Medal at the White House
Kay Ryan with family and friends
Library of Congress, hanging of Kay Ryan's portrait
The New Yorker , 75th Anniversary Reading, Chicago (with negatives)
The New Yorker Festival, New York City
Poet's House, New York City, Grand Opening (printed)
Poetry Foundation editor's luncheon, New York City (with negatives)
Portraits of Kay Ryan (for Dragon Acts to Dragon Ends , taken 1983)
Portraits of Kay Ryan (taken by Matt Valentine)
Portraits of Kay Ryan (taken by Sydney Goldstein)
Portraits of Kay Ryan, Library of Congress (printed from digital file)
Sydney's Dinner at the Bancroft Library

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains poetry broadsides, small-press books, newspapers, clippings, and books annotated by Ryan.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: Poetry Broadsides, Articles and Newspapers, and Annotated Books and Serials.

did.unittitle: Poetry Broadsides and Small Press Books

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains published poetry broadsides from various limited printings and events. Most broadsides feature Ryan's poetry, but some broadsides with poems of other writers such as Jane Hirshfield, Billy Collins, and Joseph Stroud, are also included, as well as two small press books containing Ryan's poetry and illustrated by others.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title, with broadsides of other poets filed at the end of the sequence.

did.unittitle: "All the Months Have Thirty-One Days" broadside (Jungle Garden Press)
"The Best of It" broadside (Poetry at Top Round)
"Chinese Foot Chart" broadside (San Francisco Center for the Book)
"Dew," "Hide and Seek," and "Ideal Audience" broadside (Iota Press, Volume 1, Number 5)
"The Edges of Time" broadside (SFCB Printing Poetry Press)
"The Edges of Time" broadside ([Romanée types?], for "Exploring Form and Narrative," West Chester, PA)
"Home to Roost" broadside (Steracle Press, for the Poetry Foundation)
"How Birds Sing," illustrated by Tucker Nichols, small plate book (San Francisco Center for the Book)
"Lighthouse Keeping" broadside (Ugly Duckling Presse, for Poet's House)
"Masterworks of Ming," illustrated by Nancy Allyn Jarzonbek, booklet
"New Rooms" electronic broadside (Aesop, for "O, Miami Poetry Festival")

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This broadside once featured an electronic recording of the poem "New Rooms" that played when a button was pushed.

did.unittitle: "Still Life With Lemons, Oranges, and a Rose (1633) - Francisco de Zurbaran" broadside (Julie Holcomb Printers)
Hoyem, Andrew, "The First Poet Travels To The Moon" (Arion Press)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes the broadsides "Silence" by Billy Collins, "Apollo" by Elizabeth Alexander, "Mexicans in France" by Sandra Cisneros, "From 'September Notebook: Stories'" by Robert Hass, "Poor Angels" by Edward Hirsch, and "It Was a Bichon Frisé's Life..." by Ange Mlinko.

did.unittitle: Stroud, Joseph, "Homage to the Presence of the Mystery Among Us" (Digital Letterpress)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains newspapers, clippings, and printed-out articles. Most articles feature or mention Ryan. Includes some printouts of electronically published poems, and well as nine issues of Artists Dialogue , the newspaper of the Marin Arts Council, in which Ryan published a number of articles.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by file title.

did.unittitle: Articles on Kay Ryan, "Discovering Kay Ryan," by Dana Gioia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These issues contain articles written by Kay Ryan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These issues contain articles written by Kay Ryan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This issue contains a strip from comic The Boondocks by artist Aaron McGruder that includes Kay Ryan's poem "Patience."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains print volumes owned and annotated by Ryan.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by author or publication.

did.unittitle: Brown, Kurt (editor), The Measured Word: On poetry and science
Costello, Bonnie (editor), The Selected Letters of Marianne Moore
The Georgia Review , "The Nobel Laureates of Literature: An Olympic Gathering"
Jarrell, Randall, Poetry and the Age
Jolley, Elizabeth, The Newspaper of Claremont Street

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains computer media, including flash-drives, rewritable computer disks and DVDs, and a floppy disk, as well as analog and commercially produced materials such as computer disks, DVDs, and an audiocassette.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Computer Media, and Audiovisual Media.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 40, folder 1480-1501 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 41, folder 1502-1509 (audiovisual media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copy may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes an audiocassette from Alice Quinn at The New Yorker and DVDs featuring interviews with Ryan and recordings of events in which she participated.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title.

did.unittitle: "Critical Thinking," with guest Kay Ryan (98.7 WFMT Chicago), computer disk
"An Evening with Kay Ryan, U.S. Poet Laureate" (College of Marin), DVD
"Literary Friendships, Program #3," (Minnesota Public Radio), computer disk
The New Yorker , accent tape with Alice Quinn, audiocassette
"The Poet's View: Intimate Film Profiles of Five Major American Poets" (The Academy of American Poets), DVD
"[West Chester University] Poetry Salon, Volume One: Kay Ryan," computer disk

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 42 (student records): Restricted until 2085. For further information, consult appropriate curator.
Box 43 (legal, medical, and financial records): Restricted until 2035. For further information, consult appropriate curator.
